findings.title: Caller-controlled router target and calldata let anyone drain third-party ERC20 allowances

best_agent_match.title: Caller-controlled router target and calldata let anyone drain third-party ERC20 allowances

rejected_candidates.title: Integrator identity is caller-supplied, enabling impersonation of trusted integrators
rejected_candidates.reason: The local source only shows that an `integrator` address is passed through calldata in the exploit harness; it does not demonstrate that this value is authenticated, privileged, or materially changes authorization. The harmful behavior is already explained by the arbitrary router call itself.

rejected_candidates.title: The native-call entrypoint can be invoked with zero input and zero recipient checks, turning it into a free arbitrary-call gadget
rejected_candidates.reason: This is not a distinct root cause from the confirmed arbitrary-call bug. The zeroed asset/recipient fields shown in the PoC are consequences of the same issue and do not independently demonstrate additional protocol harm beyond the allowance-drain finding.
